name: exit_policy_evolution
purpose: Evolutionary search for a regime- AND trade-age-conditioned forex exit policy. A policy maps (vix_tercile 0-2, atr_tercile 0-2, hold_count_bucket early/mid/late) -> (stop_atr_mult, trailing_atr_mult, hold_limit), evaluated per-bar as a trade ages. Generalises HYP-066 (a 2x2 median split that returned NOT_SIGNIFICANT) to a 3x3x3 grid searched by a genetic algorithm under a CPCV-Sharpe fitness. Meta-labeling (AFML ch.3): the primary model (carry entries) is FIXED to the canonical v015 decade trades; only the secondary exit policy is evolved.
mechanism: Optimal stop/trail width and hold horizon may depend BOTH on the volatility regime (VIX x ATR percentile) and on how long a trade has already been open (early/mid/late). A static or entry-only-conditioned config leaves this on the table. If true, a per-bar regime-and-age-keyed policy beats the static config out-of-sample AND survives the multiple-testing correction for the genetic search.
prior_expectation: Strongly leaning NOT_SIGNIFICANT / NOT_ROBUST. HYP-066's 2x2 entry-regime split already failed (permutation p=0.605, 0/4 BH). A 27-cell GA over ~408 decade trades (~15 trades/cell) has a far larger overfitting surface; the deflated-Sharpe hurdle (n_trials = GA search size) and the locked 2025-2026 holdout are calibrated to catch in-sample optimisation. An honest null is the expected and acceptable result. This study is GATED: it only runs against HYP-066 if HYP-066 returned VALID_EDGE; otherwise it runs only under --standalone as an independent pre-registered hypothesis.

gate.rule: Default run HALTS unless HYP-066 verdict == VALID_EDGE. --standalone overrides the gate and runs this as an independent hypothesis with a stricter (search-size) deflated-Sharpe correction.

design.entry_universe: the canonical v015 static-config decade trades (logs/forex_backtest_trades.json) - fixed primary-model output; the exit policy is the only thing varied

axes.vix_tercile: trailing-252d ^VIX percentile, binned into terciles at the decade 1/3 and 2/3 thresholds (recorded in results)
axes.atr_tercile: trailing-252d per-pair ATR percentile, binned into terciles at the decade 1/3 and 2/3 thresholds

policy.evaluation: PER-BAR: each bar the active cell is (current vix_tercile, current atr_tercile, current hold_count_bucket); stop_atr_mult and trailing_atr_mult are read from that cell each bar (decide_exit takes cfg per call). hold_limit and the initial ATR stop are read ONCE from the ENTRY cell and held fixed for the trade (PositionState.hold_limit). decide_exit itself is the SAME function live+backtest use - parity preserved.
policy.min_cell_trades_fallback: any cell with fewer than 30 bar-visits across the decade falls back to its coarser parent: collapse the hold axis (average over populated hold buckets within the vix x atr cell), then fall back to the global config. Empty cells are never noise-fit.

param_bounds.discretisation: stop/trail snapped to 0.05; hold_limit snapped to integer

static_baseline.note: canonical v015 config; per-pair trailing overrides from ForexBacktester.PAIR_TRAILING_OVERRIDES

design.exit_evaluation: single-trade exit replay via sovereign.forex.exit_machine.decide_exit from each fixed entry over the pair price/atr/signal path, no re-entry/pyramiding; costs applied via the ForexBacktester cost model (spread+slippage+swap). Reconciled separately against the canonical sequential backtest (0.6886 +/- 0.01).

genetic_algorithm.init: 80% random within param_bounds, 20% seeded from HYP-066 decade_best_cfg (4 entries) mapped 2x2 -> 3x3 (tercile 0 -> lo, 2 -> hi, 1 -> nearest; hold axis broadcast)
genetic_algorithm.fitness: CPCV Sharpe with robustness penalty: mean(path_sharpes) - 0.3 * std(path_sharpes), computed on the TRAIN window only
genetic_algorithm.crossover: uniform (each cell-param drawn independently from parent A or B)

mutation.sigma: 0.2 * param_range, Gaussian, clipped to bounds then snapped to grid

genetic_algorithm.early_stop: best fitness unchanged for 10 consecutive generations
genetic_algorithm.determinism: fully reproducible under the fixed seed

cpcv.purge: AFML interval-overlap purge at trade granularity (sovereign.discovery.cpcv.combinatorial_purged_splits); path sharpe computed on each test combination's pooled trades under the fixed policy

train.use: GA, CPCV fitness, in-sample best-cell selection - the genetic search NEVER sees the holdout

holdout.use: one-shot forward non-degradation gate AND out-of-sample permutation/BH; never used to choose configs

validation_protocol.cpcv_fitness: GA objective; reported as winner mean/std path Sharpe on train
validation_protocol.forward_holdout: winner pooled costed Sharpe on 2025-2026 vs static; non_degrade iff winner >= static - 0.05

permutation.null: the 27-cell config mapping carries no out-of-sample information
permutation.method: shuffle the winner's 27 cell-config assignments; recompute the winner's pooled HOLDOUT (2025-2026) Sharpe. Permuting on the holdout (which the GA never saw) is an honest OOS test of the conditioning structure, not an in-sample re-confirmation.

validation_protocol.multiple_trials: deflated_sharpe_ratio applied to the winner's pooled train Sharpe at n_trials = number of UNIQUE policies evaluated by the GA (the true search size) - require deflated_sr > 0
validation_protocol.fdr: benjamini_hochberg across the 9 (vix x atr) marginal cells' holdout permutation p-values (marginals, not the 27 thin cells, for FDR power), alpha=0.05 - require >=1 survivor
validation_protocol.reconcile_guard: canonical ForexBacktester(2015-2024).backtest_all() weighted_portfolio_sharpe must equal 0.6886 +/- 0.01 or HALT before any GA work (same number prove.py reports)

prove_gate.method: winner's fixed-entry costed replay weighted-portfolio Sharpe must land inside the documented bands
prove_gate.note: Two-sided RECONCILIATION bands per the spec: a winner whose honest replay Sharpe lands OUTSIDE the band (especially above) is the overfit tell -> halt + log NOT_ROBUST. The oos_band reflects the sized/sequential v015 number (CLAUDE.md), which differs from the fixed-entry replay methodology used here; the replay static baseline (winner vs static, both replay) is recorded in results so the band can be recalibrated and re-signed if the gate is to be used as a one-sided non-degradation check later.

success_criteria: VALID_EDGE iff ALL: holdout permutation p < 0.05 AND deflated_sr > 0 at n_trials = unique policies evaluated AND >=1 (vix x atr) marginal survives Benjamini-Hochberg AND 2025-2026 winner Sharpe >= static - 0.05 (non-degradation) AND prove bands cleared.
failure_criteria: NOT_ROBUST if the prove bands are missed (evolutionary loop overfit). NOT_SIGNIFICANT if any other gate fails. Either way the hypothesis is killed and logged; static config stays; no live deployment.
deployment_if_valid: NONE without Colin's explicit sign-off. A valid policy would deploy as a per-bar cfg lookup in the L2 exit manager via a separate logged param_change, never bundled with the SHADOW->live flip.
hash_method: sha256 of json.dumps(doc, sort_keys=True, separators=(',',':')) where doc = this object MINUS the hash_lock field
